content:
This is the laptop price in Nepal category where we have listed all the laptops available in Nepal along with their price. Laptops usually are available in Nepal via two mediums: Grey and Official distribution. Grey Laptops are unauthorized products with no official warranty. Likewise, official Laptops are sold via authorized distributors and come with an official warranty. However, most users opt for Grey laptops because they are marginally cheaper than the authorized laptops, which cost around 10-15% more. Advertisement Apple Macbook Price in Nepal Apple's Macbook series houses many of the best laptops that you can buy today. With the new Apple silicon chips based on ARM architecture, these machines are faster, more efficient, and more reliable than ever. Likewise, the new M4-powered Macbook Air and MacBook Pro are also already available in Nepal. MacBook Pro M5 Pro 16-inch (48/2TB) Rs. 833,000 Lenovo Laptops Price in Nepal Lenovo laptops in Nepal are known to have sturdy and lightweight designs. They offer reliable performance and are mostly catered to business professionals and students. The IdeaPad series is really popular among students in Nepal. On the other hand, the ThinkPad series is a winner in an enterprise setting. The popularity of Legion and the LOQ series in the gaming space is also quite apparent in 2025. The Flex and Yoga series is for anyone looking for a versatile device. However, they are more popular among artists and designers. in Nepal Acer laptops in Nepal include a wide range of devices for business professionals, students, and gamers. However, the brand is most popular for its budget lineups like the Nitro for Gaming and the Aspire series for students. In addition, it has the Predator series which offers top-of-the-line specs for gamers. There is also a Swift series for anyone looking for a robust yet portable laptop. 1TB, 16” QHD+ IPS) ₨ 320,000 Razor Laptop Price in Nepal Razer is a global gaming hardware brand best known for its high-performance laptops, peripherals, and bold design. Currently, Razer has only its Exclusive Razer Blade series designed for high performance gaming and content creation. The Trend of Laptops in Nepal With evolving tech, laptops have become a very handy tech for students and others. Be it for entertainment and multimedia use or getting school/college/work projects done, laptops have become an integral part of the present time. The rising trend of laptops in Nepal is directly proportional to the price. Laptops have gotten way cheaper than what you would find a decade back. We have entry-level laptops, thin ultrabook laptops, and total gaming machines available in Nepal. And people are buying them as per their needs and suit. If you are one of those folks who prefers to use a tablet instead of a laptop, check out : Cheap vs Midrange vs Gaming vs Premium Laptops As with every other consumer electronics, laptops can also be categorized into various segments as per the price and specs. We have all the major laptop brands in Nepal such as Dell, Asus, Acer, HP, Apple, and Lenovo in Nepal. Here, we have segregated the segment into entry-level, midrange, gaming, and premium categories. Let’s begin with the entry-level segment first. To put in numbers, laptops costing under 55,000 rupees are considered as the cheap/entry-level segment. You can find such laptops starting at the 30K segment with basic Intel Celeron processors with 4GB of RAM. Going up to the 40K lineup, you will also get Intel i3 / Ryzen 3 laptops that offer better performance than the Celeron ones. Moving above 50K, sometimes good laptop deals can get you Ryzen 5 laptops as well. Getting to the midrange segment (Under 1 lakh), one can get multiple options from multiple brands. Here, U/P/H Series Intel Core i5, and AMD Ryzen 5 processors are pretty common with 8GB of RAM and 256/512GB of SSD storage. You might also find discrete graphics cards like the MX550, GTX 1650, or even RTX 2050 in some laptops here. Both traditional and Ultrabook laptops are available here. The latter one is preferred for those having to commute daily. Now, let’s talk about the gaming segment, which is very popular amongst youngsters. Gaming laptops in Nepal generally start at 1 lakh and go beyond 3 lakhs, depending on the specs and configurations. The 13th Gen Core i5/ Ryen 5 + RTX 3060 combo is the cheapest while the price starts increasing with specs amping up. We have also started getting 14th Gen Intel powered laptops with flagship RTX 4080 GPU and it will continue adding in the coming times. Coming to the premium ultrabook segment (above 1 lakh rupees) you’ll find gorgeous-looking thin laptops with powerful CPUs and graphics cards. As it is designed for creative workflow and editing, you can also get some with flexible and touchscreen displays. Apple’s Macbook is pretty popular here while the Dell XPS, Asus Zenbook, and Acer Swift Go are also popular choices. The RAW CPU and GPU power are slightly toned down than of the gaming laptops here. But the design and build side of things gets more premium to compare. Where to buy laptops at the best price in Nepal? Laptops are available in both official and unofficial channels in Nepal. You might get it at a cheaper price in the latter one, but as always, we recommend you to buy one from an authorized distributors where you can get laptops at best price. It will reflect a lot on after-sales support and genuineness. Meanwhile, you can browse through hundreds of options on Gadgetbyte with their buying links, to make it easy. Always make sure of the product and buying place, before making a purchase. How much to spend on a laptop in Nepal? Well, it all comes down to your needs and preferences. If you are looking for a laptop for a school-going student, cheap laptops under 50K can do the job, while opting for a 60K segment laptop would be better. Meanwhile, those seeking for office work and college projects, you’ll need more CPU power. So, the midrange segment under 1 lakh with Core i5 /Ryzen 5 CPU would suit you. Gamers can go for gaming laptops without a doubt and content creators can opt for the premium ultrabooks as per their needs. Basically, you should first check the specs and match them to your requirements to get an ideal laptop for your needs. Laptop Price in Nepal: Conclusion Laptop price in Nepal differs from one laptop brand to another. Plus, the price of laptops in Nepal tends to fluctuate due to deal offers and competition. Some brands may offer better laptops with better specs at cheaper prices than others. So, We suggest you to look for every available option with proper market research and then only proceed to buy a laptop. To make it easier for you, Gadgetbyte is always there with the updated price list of laptops and laptop review videos as well. And if you are planning to setup a workplace at your own space make sure to check our Monitors Price In Nepal list.

However, most users opt for Grey laptops because they are marginally cheaper than the authorized laptops, which cost around 10-15% more. The Trend of Laptops in Nepal With evolving tech, laptops have become a very handy tech for students and others. Be it for entertainment and multimedia use or getting school/college/work projects done, laptops have become an integral part of the present time. The rising trend of laptops in Nepal is directly proportional to the price. Laptops have gotten way cheaper than what you would find a decade back. We have entry-level laptops, thin ultrabook laptops, and total gaming machines available in Nepal. And people are buying them as per their needs and suit. If you are one of those folks who prefers to use a tablet instead of a laptop, check out : Cheap vs Midrange vs Gaming vs Premium Laptops As with every other consumer electronics, laptops can also be categorized into various segments as per the price and specs. We have all the major laptop brands in Nepal such as Dell, Asus, Acer, HP, Apple, and Lenovo in Nepal. Here, we have segregated the segment into entry-level, midrange, gaming, and premium categories. Let’s begin with the entry-level segment first. To put in numbers, laptops costing under 55,000 rupees are considered as the cheap/entry-level segment. You can find such laptops starting at the 30K segment with basic Intel Celeron processors with 4GB of RAM. Going up to the 40K lineup, you will also get Intel i3 / Ryzen 3 laptops that offer better performance than the Celeron ones. Moving above 50K, sometimes good laptop deals can get you Ryzen 5 laptops as well. Getting to the midrange segment (Under 1 lakh), one can get multiple options from multiple brands. Here, U/P/H Series Intel Core i5, and AMD Ryzen 5 processors are pretty common with 8GB of RAM and 256/512GB of SSD storage. You might also find discrete graphics cards like the MX550, GTX 1650, or even RTX 2050 in some laptops here. Both traditional and Ultrabook laptops are available here. The latter one is preferred for those having to commute daily. Now, let’s talk about the gaming segment, which is very popular amongst youngsters. Gaming laptops in Nepal generally start at 1 lakh and go beyond 3 lakhs, depending on the specs and configurations. The 13th Gen Core i5/ Ryen 5 + RTX 3060 combo is the cheapest while the price starts increasing with specs amping up. We have also started getting 14th Gen Intel powered laptops with flagship RTX 4080 GPU and it will continue adding in the coming times. Coming to the premium ultrabook segment (above 1 lakh rupees) you’ll find gorgeous-looking thin laptops with powerful CPUs and graphics cards. As it is designed for creative workflow and editing, you can also get some with flexible and touchscreen displays. Apple’s Macbook is pretty popular here while the Dell XPS, Asus Zenbook, and Acer Swift Go are also popular choices. The RAW CPU and GPU power are slightly toned down than of the gaming laptops here. But the design and build side of things gets more premium to compare. Where to buy laptops at the best price in Nepal? Laptops are available in both official and unofficial channels in Nepal. You might get it at a cheaper price in the latter one, but as always, we recommend you to buy one from an authorized distributors where you can get laptops at best price. It will reflect a lot on after-sales support and genuineness. Meanwhile, you can browse through hundreds of options on Gadgetbyte with their buying links, to make it easy. Always make sure of the product and buying place, before making a purchase. How much to spend on a laptop in Nepal? Well, it all comes down to your needs and preferences. If you are looking for a laptop for a school-going student, cheap laptops under 50K can do the job, while opting for a 60K segment laptop would be better. Meanwhile, those seeking for office work and college projects, you’ll need more CPU power. So, the midrange segment under 1 lakh with Core i5 /Ryzen 5 CPU would suit you. Gamers can go for gaming laptops without a doubt and content creators can opt for the premium ultrabooks as per their needs. Basically, you should first check the specs and match them to your requirements to get an ideal laptop for your needs. Laptop Price in Nepal: Conclusion Laptop price in Nepal differs from one laptop brand to another. Plus, the price of laptops in Nepal tends to fluctuate due to deal offers and competition. Some brands may offer better laptops with better specs at cheaper prices than others. So, We suggest you to look for every available option with proper market research and then only proceed to buy a laptop. To make it easier for you, Gadgetbyte is always there with the updated price list of laptops and laptop review videos as well. And if you are planning to setup a workplace at your own space make sure to check our Monitors Price In Nepal list.

This is the laptop price in Nepal category where we have listed all the laptops available in Nepal along with their price. Laptops usually are available in Nepal via two mediums: Grey and Official distribution. Grey Laptops are unauthorized products with no official warranty. Likewise, official Laptops are sold via authorized distributors and come with an official warranty. However, most users opt for Grey laptops because they are marginally cheaper than the authorized laptops, which cost around 10-15% more.

Acer laptops in Nepal include a wide range of devices for business professionals, students, and gamers. However, the brand is most popular for its budget lineups like the Nitro for Gaming and the Aspire series for students. In addition, it has the Predator series which offers top-of-the-line specs for gamers. There is also a Swift series for anyone looking for a robust yet portable laptop.

Razer is a global gaming hardware brand best known for its high-performance laptops, peripherals, and bold design. Currently, Razer has only its Exclusive Razer Blade series designed for high performance gaming and content creation.

With evolving tech, laptops have become a very handy tech for students and others. Be it for entertainment and multimedia use or getting school/college/work projects done, laptops have become an integral part of the present time. The rising trend of laptops in Nepal is directly proportional to the price. Laptops have gotten way cheaper than what you would find a decade back. We have entry-level laptops, thin ultrabook laptops, and total gaming machines available in Nepal. And people are buying them as per their needs and suit.

With evolving tech, laptops have become a very handy tech for students and others. Be it for entertainment and multimedia use or getting school/college/work projects done, laptops have become an integral part of the present time. The rising trend of laptops in Nepal is directly proportional to the price. Laptops have gotten way cheaper than what you would find a decade back.

We have entry-level laptops, thin ultrabook laptops, and total gaming machines available in Nepal. And people are buying them as per their needs and suit.

As with every other consumer electronics, laptops can also be categorized into various segments as per the price and specs. We have all the major laptop brands in Nepal such as Dell, Asus, Acer, HP, Apple, and Lenovo in Nepal. Here, we have segregated the segment into entry-level, midrange, gaming, and premium categories. Let’s begin with the entry-level segment first. To put in numbers, laptops costing under 55,000 rupees are considered as the cheap/entry-level segment. You can find such laptops starting at the 30K segment with basic Intel Celeron processors with 4GB of RAM. Going up to the 40K lineup, you will also get Intel i3 / Ryzen 3 laptops that offer better performance than the Celeron ones. Moving above 50K, sometimes good laptop deals can get you Ryzen 5 laptops as well. Getting to the midrange segment (Under 1 lakh), one can get multiple options from multiple brands. Here, U/P/H Series Intel Core i5, and AMD Ryzen 5 processors are pretty common with 8GB of RAM and 256/512GB of SSD storage. You might also find discrete graphics cards like the MX550, GTX 1650, or even RTX 2050 in some laptops here. Both traditional and Ultrabook laptops are available here. The latter one is preferred for those having to commute daily. Now, let’s talk about the gaming segment, which is very popular amongst youngsters. Gaming laptops in Nepal generally start at 1 lakh and go beyond 3 lakhs, depending on the specs and configurations. The 13th Gen Core i5/ Ryen 5 + RTX 3060 combo is the cheapest while the price starts increasing with specs amping up. We have also started getting 14th Gen Intel powered laptops with flagship RTX 4080 GPU and it will continue adding in the coming times. Coming to the premium ultrabook segment (above 1 lakh rupees) you’ll find gorgeous-looking thin laptops with powerful CPUs and graphics cards. As it is designed for creative workflow and editing, you can also get some with flexible and touchscreen displays. Apple’s Macbook is pretty popular here while the Dell XPS, Asus Zenbook, and Acer Swift Go are also popular choices. The RAW CPU and GPU power are slightly toned down than of the gaming laptops here. But the design and build side of things gets more premium to compare.

Laptops are available in both official and unofficial channels in Nepal. You might get it at a cheaper price in the latter one, but as always, we recommend you to buy one from an authorized distributors where you can get laptops at best price. It will reflect a lot on after-sales support and genuineness. Meanwhile, you can browse through hundreds of options on Gadgetbyte with their buying links, to make it easy. Always make sure of the product and buying place, before making a purchase.

Well, it all comes down to your needs and preferences. If you are looking for a laptop for a school-going student, cheap laptops under 50K can do the job, while opting for a 60K segment laptop would be better. Meanwhile, those seeking for office work and college projects, you’ll need more CPU power. So, the midrange segment under 1 lakh with Core i5 /Ryzen 5 CPU would suit you. Gamers can go for gaming laptops without a doubt and content creators can opt for the premium ultrabooks as per their needs. Basically, you should first check the specs and match them to your requirements to get an ideal laptop for your needs.

Laptop price in Nepal differs from one laptop brand to another. Plus, the price of laptops in Nepal tends to fluctuate due to deal offers and competition. Some brands may offer better laptops with better specs at cheaper prices than others. So, We suggest you to look for every available option with proper market research and then only proceed to buy a laptop. To make it easier for you, Gadgetbyte is always there with the updated price list of laptops and laptop review videos as well.
